codeReview.summary: Reviewed feature metadata, handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit 5911931. The optimization panel UI, loading state, tabs, char-count coloring, and save/publish actions are implemented, but source-platform attribution is hardcoded to Instagram instead of being passed from the generator context.

issues.description: GenerateOutput renders `<OptimizePanel content={text} />` without a source platform, so OptimizePanel falls back to `sourcePlatform = 'instagram'`. This causes both `/api/optimize` requests and saved posts (`source_platform`) to be tagged as Instagram regardless of the actual originating context, violating the requirement to save variants with correct source_platform.
`variant_group_id` is generated inside `handleSaveAsPost` for each click, so saving multiple variants from one optimization run produces different group IDs instead of a stable shared group.

sharedStateObservations.observation: UI verification for protected routes can be blocked when auth/session setup is unavailable, but feature runs still mark `followedProcedure: true` after only build/lint checks.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff for session 2170de79-7046-4af0-b939-8c958f25c8ad reports agent-browser was blocked on `/login` 'Checking session...', yet skillFeedback.followedProcedure remained true.

summary: Fail. The feature is mostly implemented, but source_platform handling is incorrect because the optimize panel defaults to Instagram due missing source context wiring.
